question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here is the complete structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm, with all required and commonly used fields explained:

---

## `charmcraft.yaml` — Complete Kubernetes Charm Example

```yaml
# ─── Identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-app-k8s
type: charm                        # Required: "charm" or "bundle"
title: My Application (K8s)        # Human-readable display name
summary: A sidecar charm for my-app on Kubernetes.   # Max 78 chars
description: |
  A longer description of what this charm does,
  how it works, and any important operational details.
  This text appears on Charmhub.

# ─── Base OS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
base: ubuntu@24.04                 # OS for both build and runtime

# ─── Kubernetes-Specific Requirements ────────────────────────────────────────
assumes:
  - k8s-api                        # Requires a Kubernetes substrate
  - juju >= 3.6.0                  # Minimum Juju version

# ─── Containers (REQUIRED for K8s sidecar charms) ────────────────────────────
containers:
  app:
    resource: app-oci-image        # Must match a key in `resources`
    mounts:
      - storage: data              # Must match a key in `storage`
        location: /var/lib/app
    uid: 1000                      # Enables rootless execution (Juju 3.6+)
    gid: 1000

# ─── OCI Image Resources (REQUIRED for each container) ───────────────────────
resources:
  app-oci-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the main application container
    upstream-source: ghcr.io/example/my-app:latest  # Default/upstream image

# ─── Charm Execution Privilege (K8s only, Juju 3.6+) ─────────────────────────
charm-user: non-root               # Values: root | sudoer | non-root

# ─── Storage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data storage for the application
    location: /var/lib/app
    minimum-size: 1G
    read-only: false

# ─── Relations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
provides:
  http-api:
    interface: http

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: true
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  app-peers:
    interface: my-app-peers
    scope: global

# ─── Configuration Options ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: INFO
      description: "Log verbosity level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)"
    replicas:
      type: int
      default: 1
      description: "Number of application replicas"
    enable-tls:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: "Enable TLS termination"
    db-connection-string:
      type: secret                  # Juju secret reference
      description: "Juju secret ID for the database connection string"

# ─── Actions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate application credentials.
    params:
      force:
        type: boolean
        description: Force rotation even if not yet expired.
    additionalProperties: false

  create-backup:
    description: Create a manual backup of application data.
    params:
      label:
        type: string
        description: A label to identify this backup.
    required: [label]
    additionalProperties: false

# ─── External Charm Libraries ────────────────────────────────────────────────
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"

# ─── Build Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# ─── Links & Metadata ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
links:
  contact: maintainer@example.com
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-k8s-docs
  issues: https://github.com/example/my-app-k8s/issues
  source: https://github.com/example/my-app-k8s
  website: https://example.com
```

---

## Key Field Reference

### 🔴 Required Fields

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `name` | Charm identifier used for deployment & Charmhub URL |
| `type` | Must be `charm` |
| `base` | OS for build and runtime (e.g. `ubuntu@24.04`) |
| `summary` | One-line description (≤ 78 chars) |
| `description` | Long-form description shown on Charmhub |
| `containers` | **K8s only** — declares sidecar containers |
| `resources` | OCI image resource for each container |

### 🟡 Strongly Recommended for K8s

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `assumes` | Pre-deployment validation — declare `k8s-api` and min Juju version |
| `charm-user` | Privilege level for hook execution (`non-root`, `sudoer`, `root`) |

### 🟢 Optional but Common

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `config` | User-facing configuration knobs |
| `actions` | Runnable operations (e.g. backup, rotate) |
| `provides` / `requires` / `peers` | Integration endpoints with other charms |
| `storage` | Persistent volumes mounted into containers |
| `charm-libs` | External libraries from Charmhub |
| `parts` | Controls how `charmcraft pack` builds the charm |
| `links` | URLs for docs, issues, source, website |

---

## Important Notes

1. **`containers` ↔ `resources` are paired** — every container must reference an `oci-image` resource by name, and every such resource must have a corresponding container.

2. **`storage` ↔ `containers.mounts` are paired** — a mount's `storage` key must match a top-level `storage` entry.

3. **`charmcraft.yaml` is the single source of truth** — Charmcraft auto-generates `metadata.yaml`, `config.yaml`, and `actions.yaml` from it during `charmcraft pack`. Do not maintain those files separately.

4. **Naming convention** — Kubernetes charms are conventionally suffixed with `-k8s` (e.g. `postgresql-k8s`, `my-app-k8s`).

5. **`charm-user: non-root`** requires setting `uid`/`gid` on your containers and is only available on Juju 3.6+.
